title: melica scabrosa subsp. radula
intro: Melica scabrosa subsp. radula, also known as Rough Melic grass, is a subspecies of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a traditional medicinal plant used in herbal medicine, particularly in European folk remedies, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melica scabrosa subsp. radula include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin irritations. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
